steps.title: Identify the central methodological flaw Text 2 targets
steps.content: Text 2 emphasizes that studies of entrepreneurial training fundamentally lack control groups—they fail to compare trained participants against an untrained comparison group, which is necessary to establish whether training caused the observed results.

steps.title: Match this concern to what Text 1 leaves unanswered
steps.content:
Text 2’s critique includes “failures to establish pretraining baselines for the measured attributes of participants.” To evaluate whether Cruz’s study has that flaw, the Text 2 author needs to know whether attributes were measured at the right time.

Option A asks whether Cruz and colleagues measured attributes after the training, which Text 1 reports as outcomes but does not directly clarify in terms of measurement timing.

The other options address details (sample size, participant characteristics, comparison to entrepreneurs) that Text 2 does not specifically target as the unaddressed gap in Text 1.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
